key_insight: A configurable chemical validity operator turns oxidation-state heuristics into a tunable prior that screens and guides generative inorganic materials models toward more plausible compositions.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more reliable ultra-high-throughput materials discovery by filtering unrealistic candidates while retaining promising low-energy compounds for downstream evaluation.
method: Built on SMACT with a data-informed oxidation-state model and adjustable thresholds.
Benchmarked six state-of-the-art generative models for inorganic crystals.
Used the operator both for post-generation filtering and as a reinforcement-learning reward for latent diffusion generation.
evidence_signal: Across six generative models, stoichiometries were often reproduced but realistic oxidation-state combinations were underrepresented; filtering removed rare oxidation-state compositions while preserving compounds near the convex hull.
watch_item: Because the operator depends on tunable chemical constraints, overly conservative settings may reject novel but valid chemistries or bias exploration toward well-observed oxidation states.
paper_title: Chemical filters for ultra-high-throughput materials screening and generation
